Patient id: PT-260228-8391, Patient name: Anika Patel, Test Name: Fasting Lipid Profile, Date of Test: 2026-02-25, Result: Total cholesterol 248 mg/dL; LDL 142 mg/dL; HDL 38 mg/dL; Triglycerides 190 mg/dL, Normal Range: Total <200 mg/dL; LDL <100 mg/dL; HDL >40 mg/dL; Triglycerides <150 mg/dL, Doctor Comments: Atherogenic lipid profile. Patient id: PT-260228-8391, Patient name: Anika Patel, Test Name: Basic Metabolic Panel (BMP), Date of Test: 2026-02-25, Result: Sodium 139 mmol/L; Potassium 4.2 mmol/L; Creatinine 1.03 mg/dL; eGFR 78 mL/min/1.73m2; Glucose (fasting) 142 mg/dL, Normal Range: Creatinine 0.6 - 1.2 mg/dL; eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2; Sodium 135-145 mmol/L; Potassium 3.5-5.1 mmol/L, Doctor Comments: Renal function stable (eGFR 78). Safe to uptitrate metformin per plan but monitor creatinine and eGFR in 3 months or sooner if symptoms. Address fasting hyperglycemia with medication adjustment and lifestyle counseling.

Patient id: PT-260301-9001, Patient name: Rahul Mehra, Test Name: Lipid Panel, Date of Test: 2026-02-14, Result: Total Cholesterol 235 mg/dL; LDL 148 mg/dL; HDL 38 mg/dL; Triglycerides 210 mg/dL, Normal Range: LDL <100 mg/dL; HDL >40 mg/dL; Triglycerides <150 mg/dL, Doctor Comments: Atherogenic lipid profile with high LDL and triglycerides. If LDL remains >70 mg/dL on high-intensity statin, consider adding ezetimibe. Recheck lipid panel in 8-12 weeks.

Patient id: PT-260301-9001, Patient name: Rahul Mehra, Test Name: Renal Function (Creatinine / eGFR), Date of Test: 2026-02-14, Result: Serum creatinine 1.18 mg/dL; eGFR 64 mL/min/1.73m2, Normal Range: Serum creatinine 0.6 - 1.2 mg/dL; eGFR >90 mL/min/1.73m2, Doctor Comments: Mildly reduced eGFR consistent with CKD stage 2-3a. Monitor renal function and electrolytes in 3 months after initiating/adjusting ACE inhibitor. Avoid nephrotoxic agents and adjust drug dosing as needed.

Patient id: PT-260301-9001, Patient name: Rahul Mehra, Test Name: Urine Albumin-to-Creatinine Ratio (UACR), Date of Test: 2026-02-14, Result: UACR 45 mg/g (microalbuminuria), Normal Range: <30 mg/g, Doctor Comments: Microalbuminuria detected, consistent with early diabetic nephropathy. ACE inhibitor (ramipril) initiation appropriate; optimize blood pressure and glycaemic control. Repeat UACR in 3 months to assess response.
